mnemonics: Use the 'R.A.P.' method to choose resources: Read, Apply, Practice
keyPoints: Official documentation and textbooks provide authoritative definitions and examples
Interactive coding platforms allow for hands-on practice with data structure implementations
Video lectures and visualizations help understand abstract concepts like tree traversals and graph algorithms
commonMistakes: Relying on a single resource type instead of using a mix of text, visual, and interactive materials
Only reading about data structures without implementing them in code
Using outdated resources that don't reflect current best practices and language features
